modality_recommendation.rationale: Proven clinical success with trastuzumab and pertuzumab, optimal half-life, established development pathway
design_considerations: Target Domain IV region for anti-dimerization activity
Consider Fc engineering for enhanced effector function
Optimize CDR regions for improved affinity over trastuzumab

alternative_modalities.rationale: Better tissue penetration, maintained binding affinity based on structural studies
alternative_modalities.use_case: If full IgG shows penetration limitations or for diagnostic applications

alternative_modalities.challenges: Stability issues, rapid clearance, limited HER2 clinical precedent
alternative_modalities.use_case: Specialized applications requiring small molecular size

not_recommended.reason: Limited clinical success for HER2 targeting, rapid clearance, stability challenges
not_recommended.exception: Only consider for research tools or specialized delivery applications

scaffold_recommendations.rationale: Well-characterized framework, good stability, proven therapeutic antibody
Modern framework with excellent developability profile

primary_objectives: Achieve binding affinity equal or superior to trastuzumab (Kd < 1 nM)
Maintain ability to prevent HER2 dimerization
Optimize for good developability (low aggregation, stable expression)
secondary_objectives: Explore novel epitopes adjacent to trastuzumab binding site
Engineer for enhanced Fc effector functions

risk_assessment.rationale: Well-validated target, extensive structural data, proven binding sites
Established therapeutic area, standard antibody format
Trastuzumab patents expired, but pertuzumab still protected

minimum_viable: Generate designs with ipSAE > 0.5 and ipTM > 0.7
Identify candidates with favorable developability profiles
Produce binders targeting validated HER2 epitopes
target_success: Achieve designs with ipSAE > 0.7 and ipTM > 0.8
Generate candidates with predicted affinity improvements over trastuzumab
Identify novel binding modes while maintaining anti-dimerization activity
stretch_goals: Engineer antibodies with enhanced effector functions
Develop binders with improved tissue penetration
Create candidates suitable for antibody-drug conjugation

immediate: Launch BoltzGen campaign with recommended parameters
Set up monitoring for design generation progress
Prepare screening pipeline for generated candidates
validation: Structure prediction of top candidates against PDB references
Interface analysis and binding mode prediction
development: Binding affinity measurements vs trastuzumab baseline
